name: Forellenschluss
image_alt: Green butterhead lettuce with red-brown speckles
description: An Austrian heirloom butterhead lettuce with beautiful green leaves heavily spotted with red-brown — the name means 'trout's back' in German. Exceptionally tender with a mild, sweet flavor.
history: An heirloom from the Austrian Tyrol documented since the 18th century. Brought to American gardens through the seed-saving community in the 1990s and quickly became a favorite for its beauty and flavor.

good.reason: Efficient space use with different root depths

seasonal_quirks: The speckled coloration intensifies in cooler weather and strong light. High temperatures reduce the spotting and increase bitterness. More heat-tolerant than many butterheads.
harvest_cues: Harvest when head is formed but before it elongates and begins to bolt. Outer leaves can be taken at any time. Full heads are 6–8 inches across.
variety_notes: Also known as 'Flashy Trout's Back' in American seed catalogs. Among the most visually stunning lettuces — excellent for markets and kitchen gardens where appearance matters.
preservation: Best eaten fresh. Store in refrigerator up to 1 week.
